title: Critical Attack Tree fragment
description: Data-driven attack-tree section rendered as the unnumbered `## Critical Attack Tree` block between the Management Summary and Section 1. The renderer produces a single `graph LR` Mermaid tree (full goal-decomposition, short `T-NNN` leaf labels), a fixed one-line explanation above it, and a one-line findings pointer below it (each leaf id → title → §8 anchor; no mitigations — those live in §9). Replaces the dormant critical-attack-chain.schema.json (2026-05 hybrid migration). This is the report's single cross-finding view — the §3.1 Attack Chain Overview was retired; §3 is per-Critical sequenceDiagram walkthroughs only.

intro.description: DEPRECATED (2026-05-30): no longer rendered — the section opens with a fixed one-line explanation. Retained as optional only so pre-2026-05-30 fragments still validate; new fragments should omit it.

root_goal.description: Short business-impact statement that names the attacker's terminal objective (e.g. 'Full admin takeover', 'Customer PII exfiltration'). Rendered as the root node label.

orientation.description: Authored value is NOT used for layout — the renderer normalizes the unified tree to `graph LR` (sibling leaves stack vertically, so one diagram stays readable at any fan-out). Retained for backward compatibility; author `TD`.

class.description: Tree-shape classes: `goal` (root), `and_node` / `or_node` (internal refinement), `leaf` (primitive finding ref). Legacy chain classes are retained for backwards compatibility during the hybrid transition.

finding_ref.description: Optional F-NNN or T-NNN backlink. Required when `class` is `leaf` so the renderer can emit a deep link into §8 Threat Register.

refinement.description: DEPRECATED (2026-05-30): the rendered AND/OR edge label is now DERIVED from the destination node's class (`or_node`→OR, `and_node`→AND), which makes a per-edge value contradicting the parent class impossible. This field is no longer read; encode AND/OR via the parent node's `class` instead.

key_takeaway.description: DEPRECATED (2026-05-30): no longer rendered — the Branch table now carries the cross-finding view. Retained as optional only for backward compatibility with pre-2026-05-30 fragments; new fragments should omit it.

mitigation_breakpoints.description: DEPRECATED (2026-05-30): no longer rendered — the Branch table's Primary Mitigation column (derived from canonical mitigations[].threat_ids) supersedes it. Retained as optional only for backward compatibility; new fragments should omit it.

stages.description: DEPRECATED (2026-05-30): no longer rendered. The section now shows a one-line findings pointer derived from the tree's leaf nodes (mitigations live in §9, not here). Retained as optional only for backward compatibility with pre-2026-05-30 fragments; new fragments should omit it.
